test_queries.query: In Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima, how did the Supreme Court reconcile parental guardianship claims with the welfare test under the Guardians and Wards Act, 1890?
test_queries.document_title: Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima and others
test_queries.expected_context: Paras 3-4 discussing Sections 25 and 17 of the Guardians and Wards Act, paramount welfare principle, and treatment of father as natural guardian subject to welfare

test_queries.document_title: Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima and others
test_queries.expected_context: Paras 3-5 emphasizing welfare over automatic parental preference, discussion of petitioner's residence abroad and conduct in proceedings, and continuity of children's care with mother

test_queries.query: If children are already living safely with their mother and studying, what factors did the Supreme Court consider before refusing to transfer custody to the father?
test_queries.document_title: Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima and others
test_queries.expected_context: Paras 2-5 factual history and welfare analysis, Section 17(2) factors such as age, capacity and existing care, findings on father's non-participation and mother's ongoing care

test_queries.query: What precedents did the Supreme Court cite in Gul Sadem Khan for the doctrine that welfare of the minor overrides strict personal law custody claims?
test_queries.document_title: Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima and others
test_queries.expected_context: Paras 3-4 citing 2008 SCMR 480, 2004 SCMR 1839, 1985 SCMR 1367, 1991 SCMR 1834 and 2000 SCMR 838 to reaffirm welfare-first custody jurisprudence

test_queries.query: Which specific statutory provisions were reproduced in the judgment, and what core custody test did the Court derive from them?
test_queries.document_title: Gul Sadem Khan v. Mst. Halima and others
test_queries.expected_context: Paragraph 3 reproduction of Section 25 and Section 17 of the Guardians and Wards Act, with the Court's articulation that welfare and betterment of minors is the paramount test
